operating_state: Total revenues increased 98% to $11.96 billion driven by 107,272 additional Model 3 and Model Y deliveries with higher average selling prices, partially offset by fewer Model S and Model X deliveries during new model transition. Energy generation and storage revenue increased 116% from higher Megapack and Powerwall deployments. Services revenue increased 95% from expanded used vehicle sales and maintenance services as fleet grew.
cost_structure: Total automotive gross margin improved from 25% to 28% driven by lower material, manufacturing, and freight costs from localized procurement in China, partially offset by higher export freight costs. Energy storage gross margin declined from 6% to 2% due to higher proportion of Solar Roof operating at lower margins during production ramp. Operating expenses increased significantly from headcount growth and stock-based compensation.
material_events: Jerome Guillen departed as President of Tesla Heavy Trucking effective June 3, 2021. Company invested $1.50 billion in bitcoin, realized $128 million gains from sales, and recorded $50 million impairment losses before suspending bitcoin payment acceptance in May 2021. Continued construction of Gigafactory Berlin and Texas with production expected to begin late 2021.
macro_exposures: Global semiconductor and component shortages required additional workaround manufacturing solutions that may be difficult to sustain. COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain challenges including port congestion, intermittent supplier delays, and increased logistics costs. Foreign exchange exposure across Chinese yuan, euro, Canadian dollar and Australian dollar with limited hedging activities affects operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures of $4.50 to $6.00 billion in 2021 and each of the next two fiscal years, with actual spend likely on the higher end in 2021. Production growth depends on successful ramp of Gigafactory Berlin and Texas facilities beginning late 2021 and development of proprietary battery cell manufacturing capabilities.
uncertainty_profile: Production plans subject to uncertainties from establishing and ramping multiple concurrent international manufacturing projects including Gigafactory Berlin operating under conditional permits pending final regulatory approvals. Digital asset holdings of $1.47 billion subject to volatile market prices requiring impairment recognition for value decreases. Ongoing government investigations and intellectual property litigation create potential for liability, penalties, licensing costs and product redesign requirements.
